rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydroalkylation of electron-poor olefins with unactivated alkyl iodides via anodic generation of alpha-aminoalkyl radicals that perform halogen-atom transfer (e-XAT)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Et3N to aminium radical cation followed by alpha-deprotonation generates an alpha-aminoalkyl radical that acts as the XAT mediator; the alkyl radical adds to the Michael acceptor and the resulting alpha-carbonyl C-radical is then reduced at the cathode to a carbanion which is protonated by H2O

key_intermediates.name: alpha-iodoamine / iminium iodide pair from XAT
alkyl radical (primary, secondary, or tertiary) from alkyl iodide
alpha-EWG carbon-centered radical after Giese addition

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Et3N to its aminium radical cation, which loses an alpha-H to give an alpha-aminoalkyl radical; this radical abstracts I from the alkyl iodide (XAT) producing an alkyl radical that adds to the electron-poor olefin, and the resulting alpha-EWG radical is reduced at the Pt cathode to a carbanion that is protonated by H2O.
rate_determining_step: Halogen-atom transfer from alkyl iodide to the alpha-aminoalkyl radical (driven by exothermic formation of iminium iodide)

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropylmethyl iodide radical clock (substrate 88)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 89 obtained in 36% yield with only 12% unrearranged 90, confirming a free alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterated product 91 in 89% yield with >95% D incorporation at the alpha-EWG position, indicating cathodic reduction to a carbanion that is protonated by water

probe_reagents.name: tethered alkene radical probe (substrate 85)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo cyclization to pyrrolidine 86 (with intermolecular Giese products 87a/87b when external acceptor added), supporting intramolecular radical addition pathway

probe_reagents.name: amine screen (Et3N, iPr2NEt, DABCO, TMP, iPr2NH, Bn3N, Ph2NPMP, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine)
probe_reagents.observation: Only alkylamines with secondary alpha-H and suitable Eox (~+0.67 to +0.77 V) gave high yields; amines lacking alpha-H gave no reaction, supporting alpha-aminoalkyl radical as the XAT agent

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Et3N vs alkyl iodides/acrylates
probe_reagents.observation: Et3N oxidizes at much lower potential than reduction of alkyl iodides or Michael acceptors, ruling out direct SET to substrate and supporting mediated XAT

notes: Authors cite prior DFT (ref 37) showing the XAT step proceeds via a polarized transition state with charge-transfer character; no new DFT performed here. Method avoids sacrificial anodes by replacing cathodic reduction of R-I with anodic-mediator XAT, with cathode reserved for the radical-polar crossover step (reduction of the Giese adduct radical to a carbanion).
